To Whom It May Concern:
I appreciate the opportunity to provide comments regarding the standards for the care of breeding
female dogs and the exercise and socialization of dogs regulated under the Animal Welfare Act
(AWA).
The current standards of care for dogs do not reflect modern science or humane expectations for
companion animals.

As a U.S. citizen and voter, I care deeply about adequate standards of care for dogs.
Every single dog raised in a USDA-regulated puppy mill deserves proper care for a healthy life.
Please take steps to quickly update the USDA's regulations to improve the standards of care for commercially bred dogs, which are long overdue.

The standards for these poor animals need to be better. We are constantly having our state's humane organizations having to rescue large numbers of breeding dogs from subpar puppy mills. Stronger laws would hopefully keep some of these low life breeders from getting into this miserable business.

Puppy mills need to be abolished. There is no plus side to having them. They create a burden on the whole state to solve problems that they create. They are cruel and inhumane and quite frankly it is embarrassing to have Missouri rank #1 in these evil things.

The treatment of these puppies/dogs are these mills NEEDS to be reconsidered as it is barely above survival for these animals. A humane approach needs to be enforced by the USDA to stop the torture of these innocent creatures being exploited for money.
